title: Acute Abdominal Pain
subtitle: Emergency Department · Triage Level 2
chiefComplaint: Abdominal pain for 12 hours, migrating to RLQ
description: A young student presents with classic migrating abdominal pain. Diagnose before complications set in — every minute matters.

title: Prolonged Fever with Abdominal Discomfort
subtitle: Medicine Ward · Day 3 of Admission
chiefComplaint: Stepwise rising fever for 10 days with headache, abdominal discomfort and loose stools
description: 10 days of stepwise rising fever, anorexia, loose stools, and subtle physical findings. Multiple tropical infections share overlapping features.

title: Crushing Chest Pain with Diaphoresis
subtitle: Emergency Department · Acute Presentation
chiefComplaint: Severe crushing central chest pain for 90 minutes radiating to the left arm and jaw, with profuse sweating
description: Sudden onset severe crushing substernal chest pain 90 minutes ago while driving. Described as a vice-like pressure (8/10 severity). No clear precipitating exertion.

title: Acute Vomiting with Abdominal Pain
subtitle: Emergency OPD → General Surgery Ward · Acute Admission
chiefComplaint: Profuse vomiting for 2 days with colicky central abdominal pain and absolute constipation
description: A middle-aged man with a history of previous appendicectomy presents with 2 days of profuse vomiting progressing to faeculent quality, absolute constipation, and worsening abdominal distension.
